seo.title: AI Agent Observability in OpenAI Agents SDK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent observability in OpenAI Agents SDK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: AI Agent Observability challenges in OpenAI Agents SDK arise from opaque handoffs between specialized agents, unvalidated tool outputs, and incomplete tracing across multi-agent workflows. These issues manifest as infinite loops during agent handoffs, guardrail failures from prompt injections, and silent data corruption from unvalidated tool responses. Without proper observability, debugging production agents becomes impossible as errors propagate invisibly through the agent hierarchy.

howItManifests.scenario: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
howItManifests.symptom: Runner enters infinite loop with repeating handoff traces in SDK logs
howItManifests.impact: 100% CPU usage, escalating OpenAI API costs, unresponsive agent

howItManifests.symptom: Guardrail agent returns 'approved' despite malicious input detected in traces
howItManifests.impact: Security vulnerabilities, data exfiltration, compliance violations

howItManifests.scenario: Tool output not validated before agent consumption
howItManifests.symptom: Malformed JSON from tools causes agent parsing errors mid-conversation
howItManifests.impact: Conversation derailment, hallucinated responses, user trust erosion

howItManifests.scenario: Tracing gaps in multi-agent handoff chains
howItManifests.symptom: Trace spans missing between handoff agents, no visibility into state transfer
howItManifests.impact: Impossible root cause analysis, blind debugging of production failures

detection.name: Handoff Loop Detector
detection.description: Scans OpenAI Agents SDK traces for repeating agent handoff patterns exceeding 5 cycles

detection.name: Guardrail Bypass Scanner
detection.description: Detects prompt injection success by analyzing guardrail input/output pairs for semantic similarity

detection.name: Tool Validation Gap Finder
detection.description: Identifies tools without JSON schema validation before agent consumption

fixes.name: Handoff Loop Breaker
fixes.description: Implement max_handoffs limit in OpenAI Agents SDK Runner configuration
fixes.code:
from openai_agents import Runner, Agent

researcher = Agent(name="Researcher", max_handoffs=3)
analyst = Agent(name="Analyst", max_handoffs=3)

runner = Runner(
    agents=[researcher, analyst],
    max_iterations=10,
    handoff_timeout=30.0  # seconds
)

result = runner.run("Analyze market trends", max_steps=50)

fixes.name: Input Sanitization Guardrail
fixes.description: Add pre-handoff input sanitization using OpenAI Agents SDK guardrails

fixes.name: Tool Output Validator
fixes.description: Enforce JSON schema validation on all tool outputs before agent consumption

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Observability Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly as an output guardrail in OpenAI Agents SDK, automatically detecting handoff loops, guardrail bypasses, and validation gaps across all agent interactions. By wrapping the Runner's output stream, Vex provides real-time observability into multi-agent workflows, capturing trace gaps and tool validation failures that traditional logging misses. 

Vex's agent-specific detectors analyze SDK trace events natively, providing precise remediation recommendations like max_handoffs limits and JSON schema enforcement. The platform auto-generates fix configurations and monitors production agents continuously, preventing observability failures before they impact users. 

Integration requires one line of code and works with existing OpenAI Agents SDK workflows without modification.
vexAutomation.code:
pip install openai-agents vex-sdk

from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail
from openai_agents import Runner, Agent

# Initialize Vex guardrail for agent observability
vex = VexGuardrail(
    detectors=[
        'handoff_loop_detector',
        'guardrail_bypass_scanner',
        'tool_validation_gap',
        'trace_completeness'
    ]
)

# Your existing agents
researcher = Agent(name="Researcher")
analyst = Agent(name="Analyst")

# Add Vex as output guardrail
runner = Runner(
    agents=[researcher, analyst],
    output_guardrails=[vex]
)

# Vex auto-detects and alerts on observability issues
result = runner.run("Complex multi-agent task")

# View Vex dashboard for issues and auto-fixes
vex.view_dashboard()
